prompt: Only one button is visible in the JFrame. What is the bug?
choices: JFrame can only hold one component
JFrame uses BorderLayout by default; adding without constraints puts both in CENTER, with B replacing A
JButton cannot be added directly to JFrame
setSize must be called before adding components
explanation: JFrame's default layout is BorderLayout. When you add() without a constraint, the component goes to BorderLayout.CENTER. Only one component fits in each region — B replaces A. Fix: use f.setLayout(new FlowLayout()) or specify constraints like f.add(btnA, BorderLayout.NORTH).
